data_in.description: chromVARDeviations object (dev) containing bias-corrected deviations and z-scores for JASPAR motifs matched to filtered peaks from 10 GM + 10 H1 example cells

data_out.description: Ranked variability data frame with motif names, standard deviation of z-scores, bootstrap confidence intervals, and p-values for variability > 1
Differential-deviations results table with per-motif statistics comparing GM vs H1 cell types (p-values, test statistics)

domain_knowledge: Variability in chromVAR is measured as the standard deviation of bias-corrected z-scores across all cells/samples for a given motif, reflecting how consistently that motif's chromatin accessibility deviates from expectation.

Bootstrap resampling in chromVAR variability testing redraws cells/samples with replacement to generate confidence intervals and assess whether observed variability exceeds the null hypothesis of variability = 1 (no effect).

The differentialDeviations function tests whether bias-corrected deviations for a motif differ significantly between groups (e.g., GM vs H1 cell types) specified by colData column annotation, identifying cell-type-specific chromatin accessibility patterns.
Bias-corrected deviations normalize for GC content and average peak accessibility, ensuring that variability reflects true biological chromatin-accessibility differences rather than technical biases.

Assess whether reported per-motif statistics in article or SI match the computational outputs in magnitude and direction (statistical and biological interpretation)

[other] Ranked variability data frame with motif names, standard deviation of z-scores, bootstrap confidence intervals, and p-values for variability > 1: 'variability <- computeVariability(dev)'
[other] Differential-deviations results table with per-motif statistics comparing GM vs H1 cell types (p-values, test statistics): 'diff_acc <- differentialDeviations(dev, "Cell_Type")'

cmd: library(chromVAR); library(SummarizedExperiment); dev <- readRDS('{inputs.chromvar_dev_object}'); var_results <- computeVariability(dev); diff_results <- differentialDeviations(dev, groups = dev$cell_type); write.csv(var_results, 'variability_rankings.csv'); write.csv(diff_results, 'differential_deviations.csv')

expected_outputs: Ranked variability data frame with motif names, standard deviation of z-scores, bootstrap confidence intervals, and p-values for variability > 1
Differential-deviations results table with per-motif statistics comparing GM vs H1 cell types (p-values, test statistics)
finding: chromVAR is designed to identify motifs or other genomic annotations associated with variability in chromatin accessibility between individual cells or samples, enabling ranking and differential analysis of motif usage.
inputs: chromVARDeviations object (dev) containing bias-corrected deviations and z-scores for JASPAR motifs matched to filtered peaks from 10 GM + 10 H1 example cells
methodology_summary: Load chromVARDeviations object containing bias-corrected motif deviations and z-scores from filtered example ATAC-seq data (10 GM + 10 H1 cells).

Apply computeVariability to rank motifs by standard deviation of z-scores across samples; compute bootstrap confidence intervals by resampling cells and test against null hypothesis of variability = 1.
Visualize ranked motif variability using plotVariability to identify high-variability motifs across the cell population.
Apply differentialDeviations to test for statistically significant differences in bias-corrected deviations between GM and H1 cell types using cell-type annotations in colData.

Validation: Verify that variability rankings match expected high-variability motifs (e.g., cell-type-specific TFs) and that differential-deviation p-values are properly computed and < 0.05 for true positives reported in the paper.
research_question: How can motifs be ranked by their variability in chromatin accessibility across cell populations, and which motifs show statistically significant differential deviation between distinct cell types?

task_description: Compute variability rankings and differential-deviation statistics for motifs using a chromVARDeviations object derived from the example 10-GM / 10-H1 dataset. Output a ranked variability table and a differential-deviation results table with per-motif statistics comparing cell types.

task_inputs.label: chromVARDeviations object with motif deviations and z-scores

task_objective: Compute variability rankings for motifs and generate differential-deviation statistics comparing GM vs H1 cell populations using chromVAR's computeVariability and differentialDeviations functions.

task_outputs.label: Ranked variability results with confidence intervals and p-values
Differential-deviation test results comparing cell types

title: Reproduce variability ranking of JASPAR motifs across GM and H1 cell lines
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: SummarizedExperiment, Ranked variability data frame with motif names, standard deviation of z-scores, bootstrap confidence intervals, and p-values for variability > 1, Differential-deviations results table with per-motif statistics comparing GM vs H1 cell types (p-values, test statistics).
workflow_description: 1. Load the pre-computed chromVARDeviations object (dev) derived from filtered example_counts with matched JASPAR motifs. 2. Call computeVariability(dev) to compute standard deviation of z-scores across samples for each motif, generate bootstrap confidence intervals by resampling cells/samples, and perform hypothesis tests against null variability of 1. 3. Rank motifs by variability score and call plotVariability(variability) to visualize rank-sorted annotations. 4. Call differentialDeviations(dev, "Cell_Type") to test for significant differences in bias-corrected deviations between GM and H1 cell groups using the colData cell-type annotation. 5. Export ranked variability results and differential-deviation test results (p-values, effect sizes per motif) as structured tables.
